description: Test the combine-ingredients edge function by simulating event scenarios (groups of 3-5 recipes), running the naive pre-combine then the AI semantic merge, and using Claude to evaluate whether ingredients were combined correctly. Iteratively improve the combine prompt based on findings.

userStories.title: Create batch-combine script
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a script that groups recipes into simulated events, pre-combines their ingredients, calls the combine-ingredients edge function, and saves results for evaluation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test-combine/scripts/batch-combine.mjs that queries all recipes and ingredients from local Supabase DB
Groups recipes into random but deterministic 'events' of 3-5 recipes each (use recipe index as seed so groups are reproducible)
For each event group: collects all ingredients from those recipes, performs a simple pre-combine (group by lowercase name + unit, sum quantities, collect sourceRecipes), formats as PreCombinedInput[]
Calls the combine-ingredients edge function (POST http://127.0.0.1:54321/functions/v1/combine-ingredients) with {preCombined} body
Auth headers: apikey and Authorization both 'REDACTED'
Accepts --offset N and --limit N flags to process a slice of event groups
Saves results to test-combine/combine-results.json: array of {groupIndex, recipes: [{name}], preCombinedCount, combinedCount, preCombined: [...], combined: [...]}
Appends to existing results file if it exists (merges by groupIndex, replacing duplicates)
Logs progress per group and prints summary (groups processed, total pre-combined items, total combined items, merge ratio)
Script runs successfully with --limit 0 (no-op): node test-combine/scripts/batch-combine.mjs --limit 0
userStories.notes: DB has 154 recipes and 1898 ingredients. ~6 recipes have 0 ingredients (broken URLs) — skip those when forming groups. The pre-combine step should be simple: lowercase name grouping + quantity sum by unit. The edge function handles semantic merging. The combine-ingredients edge function expects: {preCombined: [{name, quantity (string|null), unit (string|null), category, sourceRecipes}]}. Note quantity is a STRING (or null), formatted as fraction like '1 1/2' — see src/lib/groceryList.ts decimalToFraction(). For simplicity, just pass the numeric quantity as a string. The edge function returns {success, items: [{name, totalQuantity (number|null), unit, category, sourceRecipes}]}. Reference: test-combine/scripts/batch-parse.mjs for the fetch/auth pattern.

userStories.title: Create Claude-powered combine evaluator
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an evaluation script that uses Claude to assess whether the combine-ingredients function correctly merged ingredients for each event group.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test-combine/scripts/evaluate-combined.mjs that reads test-combine/combine-results.json
For each event group: sends the preCombined input AND the combined output to Claude for evaluation

Claude evaluates: (1) missed_merge — items that should have been merged but weren't (e.g. 'garlic' and 'garlic clove' both in output), (2) wrong_merge — items that were incorrectly merged (e.g. 'sesame oil' merged with 'olive oil'), (3) quantity_error — quantities not summed correctly after merge, (4) unit_error — wrong unit choice when merging incompatible units, (5) name_cleaning — output names should be clean base names (no prep adjectives, no count units), (6) category_error — wrong category in output, (7) source_recipes_error — sourceRecipes not correctly combined/deduplicated
Uses ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from environment or from supabase/functions/.env
Processes event groups in batches of 5 per Claude API call (combine results are larger than parsed ingredients)
Outputs to test-combine/combine-evaluation-report.json: {summary: {totalIssues, totalGroups, byType}, issues: [{groupIndex, recipes, issueType, description, inputItems, outputItem, suggestedFix}]}
Script handles empty results file gracefully
Script runs without errors when given valid combine-results.json
userStories.notes: Follow the pattern from test-combine/scripts/evaluate-parsed.mjs for structure (API key loading, Claude API call pattern, batch processing, rate limit handling, report format). Key difference: each evaluation batch contains both INPUT (preCombined) and OUTPUT (combined) so Claude can compare them. Claude should check that every input item maps to exactly one output item, quantities are preserved or correctly summed, and no information is lost.

userStories.title: Run batch 1 (groups 1-10), evaluate, and fix combine prompt
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to combine the first 10 event groups, evaluate the output, and fix the combine-ingredients prompt for common issues.
acceptanceCriteria: Run: node test-combine/scripts/batch-combine.mjs --offset 0 --limit 10
Verify 10 groups processed successfully
Run: node test-combine/scripts/evaluate-combined.mjs
Read combine-evaluation-report.json and identify ALL issue types and their frequency
Fix the combine prompt in supabase/functions/combine-ingredients/index.ts based on findings
Document in activity.md: issue counts by type, specific prompt changes made
Existing edge function tests pass: npx vitest run tests/unit/edge-functions/combine-ingredients.test.ts
Typecheck passes: npm run build
userStories.notes: This is the first real test. Expect issues like: ingredients not being merged when they should be, quantities getting lost, units being mishandled. The combine prompt (in supabase/functions/combine-ingredients/index.ts systemPrompt) is relatively simple compared to parse-recipe — there's room for improvement. Focus on the most common issue types first.

userStories.title: Run batch 2 (groups 11-20), evaluate, and fix if needed
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to combine groups 11-20 with the improved prompt and verify the fixes from batch 1 are working.
acceptanceCriteria: Run: node test-combine/scripts/batch-combine.mjs --offset 10 --limit 10
Run: node test-combine/scripts/evaluate-combined.mjs
Compare issue counts against batch 1: fixed issue types should appear less or not at all
IF new issue types found: fix the prompt in supabase/functions/combine-ingredients/index.ts
IF no new issues: document 'prompt is stable' in activity.md
Document comparison to batch 1 in activity.md
Edge function tests pass if prompt was changed
Typecheck passes: npm run build
userStories.notes: Compare directly to batch 1 results. If the issue types from US-003 are now reduced/eliminated, the prompt fixes are working.

userStories.title: Run remaining groups, evaluate, and confirm stability
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to combine all remaining event groups and confirm the prompt is stable.
acceptanceCriteria: Run: node test-combine/scripts/batch-combine.mjs --offset 20 (processes all remaining groups)
Run: node test-combine/scripts/evaluate-combined.mjs
IF new issue types found: fix the prompt and re-run affected groups
IF no new issues: document 'prompt stable' in activity.md
Document final statistics in activity.md: total groups, total issues by type, merge ratio
Edge function tests pass if prompt was changed
Typecheck passes: npm run build
userStories.notes: This processes all remaining groups. Expected ~20-30 groups depending on how many recipes have ingredients. After this, we should have full coverage of all 148 parseable recipes.

userStories.title: Full re-run all groups with final prompt and final evaluation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to re-run ALL event groups with the finalized combine prompt to ensure consistent quality across all groups.
acceptanceCriteria: Delete or clear test-combine/combine-results.json to start fresh
Run: node test-combine/scripts/batch-combine.mjs (all groups, no offset/limit)
Run: node test-combine/scripts/evaluate-combined.mjs
Total issues should be near zero — if not, make final prompt adjustments and re-run
Save final report as test-combine/combine-final-evaluation-report.json
Document in activity.md: final issue counts, comparison to batch 1 counts, overall improvement
Edge function tests pass: npx vitest run tests/unit/edge-functions/combine-ingredients.test.ts
Typecheck passes: npm run build
userStories.notes: This is the final validation. All groups re-run with the polished prompt. The evaluation should show near-zero true issues. Save the final report separately from the iterative one.
